title: Aerides obtusa
taxonomy: Aerides obtusa is classified in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. The genus Aerides is a part of this family, and it is further divided into various species based on their unique characteristics. Aerides obtusa is a species within this genus and is native to various parts of Asia. This species is known for its fragrant and showy flowers, which come in a range of colors including white, yellow, and pink. Within the classification, Aerides obtusa is often grouped alongside other species of Aerides due to their shared characteristics and adaptations.
uses: Aerides obtusa, also known as the 'Tahina', is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia. intro: Aerides obtusa, commonly known as the 'Rafflesia orchid' or 'Dendrobium', is a type of flowering orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia.
